raw_title: (Trans)Locality in Social Media News Engagement: Geographic Effects on the Quality of Online Discussion
title_en: (Trans)Locality in Social Media News Engagement: Geographic Effects on the Quality of Online Discussion
title_zh: (Trans)Locality in Social Media News Engagement: Geographic Effects on the Quality of Online Discussion
plain_summary_en: This paper studies (Trans)Locality in Social Media News Engagement: Geographic Effects on the Quality of Online Discussion. In plain terms, it asks what is happening, why it matters for public communication, and what evidence the authors use. The likely focus is Social media, Locality, Quality (philosophy), Public relations. The abstract suggests a mixed methods approach, which makes the work useful for understanding how communication changes in everyday life.

research_direction_en: The paper points to an active research direction around Social media, Locality, Quality (philosophy), Public relations, especially how institutions, platforms, and audiences shape communication outcomes.
